headline: QUEENSWAY GROWS: New Found Gold Drills 24.8 g/t Au over 14m, Dropkick Trend Now 1.4 km
snippet: New Found Gold expanded the Dropkick Zone at its Queensway Gold Project in Newfoundland to over 1.4 kilometres along strike, with standout intercepts including 24.8 g/t Au over 14.0 m from 324 m depth. The high-grade domain remains open in multiple directions and will feed an updated Queensway resource estimate targeted for H2 2026.

headline: ATHABASCA STEP-OUTS: F3 Uranium Confirms Mineralization in 3 of 6 Holes at Broach Tetra Zone
snippet: F3 Uranium intersected uranium mineralization in three of six step-out holes testing the Tetra Zone extension at its Patterson Lake North Project in Saskatchewan's Athabasca Basin, extending the footprint along strike and down plunge. Best hole returned 0.5 m radioactivity up to 1,400 cps at 470.5 m, with the broader A1/B1 corridor remaining a key target for follow-up.

headline: GOLD HOLDS $4,750: Metal Rebounds as Iran Ceasefire Extended, Second Round of Peace Talks Possible
snippet: Gold recovered to $4,752.76 per ounce on Wednesday after the US extended the ceasefire with Iran, though optimism over a second round of peace talks is fragile. The metal remains up 43% year-over-year — its best annual pace in decades — as central banks maintain record net buying at a projected 600–800 tonnes for 2026.

headline: MINE TO MAGNET: USA Rare Earth Buys Brazil's Serra Verde for $2.8B, Securing All 4 Magnetic Rare Earths Outside Asia
snippet: USA Rare Earth agreed to acquire Serra Verde Group for approximately $2.8 billion in cash and stock, gaining the Pela Ema mine in Brazil — described as the only ex-Asia operation capable of supplying all four magnetic rare earths at scale. The deal includes a 15-year government-backed offtake covering 100% of NdPr, Dy, and Tb output, with closing expected in Q3 2026.

headline: 295 MT AT 1.36% Li2O: Q2 Metals Delivers One of North America's Largest Hard-Rock Lithium Resources
snippet: Q2 Metals established an inaugural inferred resource of 295 million tonnes grading 1.36% Li2O at its Cisco project in Quebec's James Bay region, placing it among the largest hard-rock lithium deposits in the Americas. The pit-constrained portion totals 270 Mt at 1.36% Li2O, with a PEA now targeted for 2027 after continued infill drilling.

headline: BHP RAISES GUIDANCE: Record Escondida Throughput Lifts FY26 Copper Production Target to 1.9–2.0 Million Tonnes
snippet: BHP upgraded its full-year copper production guidance after Escondida, the world's largest copper mine, delivered record concentrator throughput in the nine months to March 2026. The revised target of 1.9–2.0 million tonnes underlines tightening global supply conditions at a time when copper prices are pressing two-month highs near $6.10/lb.

headline: COPPER SURGES PAST $6/LB: Futures Hit Two-Month High as Iran Ceasefire Holds and Tariff Relief Eyed
snippet: Copper futures climbed toward $6.10 per pound, fully recovering losses incurred since the start of the Iran conflict, as traders weighed a possible second round of US-Iran peace talks and reports of potential US tariff relief on Chinese goods. Smelter TC/RC margins at multi-year lows remain a structural supply headwind beneath the bullish macro narrative.

headline: DEFICIT NO. 6: Silver Near $78 as Sixth Straight Annual Supply Shortfall Deepens, Dollar Weighs
snippet: Silver is trading near $78 per ounce amid a projected sixth consecutive annual supply deficit of 46.3 million troy ounces — up 15% from 2025 — as dollar strength and uncertainty over Iran peace talks cap near-term gains. China's solar buildout continues to absorb record import volumes, keeping structural demand well above primary mine supply.

headline: WEDBUSH: MP Materials Is America's Only Fully Integrated Rare Earth Play, Price Target $90
snippet: Wedbush initiated MP Materials at Outperform with a $90 price target, arguing the market has not fully priced in the vertical integration advantages linking Mountain Pass mine to the Independence magnet facility. The mid-2026 commissioning of the heavy rare earth separation plant — which would supply Dy and Tb without Chinese processing — is cited as the next major catalyst.

headline: CORVO INAUGURAL PROGRAM COMPLETE: Aventis Energy Hits Radioactive Anomalies in Multiple Drill Holes
snippet: Aventis Energy concluded its inaugural diamond drill campaign at the Corvo Uranium Project, intersecting anomalous radioactivity across multiple holes that will inform an updated geological model and guide the next phase of exploration. Results provide early-stage confirmation of a potential uranium system and set the stage for a targeted follow-up drill program.
